name: Dean Browning
subject: The Rise of Authoritarianism
body:
Parler has been banned by Big Tech
---
| | | | | |
---
| Dear Patriot,
The social media network Parler has been banned by the Big Tech monopolies like Amazon and Apple. If you don't know what Parler is, it is a platform for free speech that is uncensored and unbiased. While so many mainstream social media platforms have diligently worked to silence and censor Conservatives, Parler stood as an outlet for all kinds of ideas, thoughts, opinions, and expressions. This ban is EXTREMELY ALARMING. This is the rise of authoritarianism at its best. It is a concentrated effort to ensure the "free-flow" of communication and information is in line with Far-Left extremists' ideals. It is clear that even the President of the United States is not exempt from the power Big Tech has over free speech and communication. We must fight back against these monopolies. We must ensure that any platform used for communication does not stifle the voices of those who express different views. Would you be willing to chip in today so we can protect our most treasured right, the First Amendment? [A contribution of $25, $15, or even just $5 will help us win this battle](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0019QiKG1J_UIKiEumv8gbwLgjVMZSLq1BTK_WdhBX3ue1GN_pk_VRsurdQNnvXqPOf359k0ZmYdFdR4t1ctA8xPKTs4oVauWkQ9qlhO2xJ_i_7peAAO7KZw709ofFvpjWTsWhG0A3cqqmEorfnIVnB_F7_lAW678CgIGO5TT-nrjxYJeSILfQ9hEFgcmfGt26B-bpsmYeN8mE=&c=Nwgf06IBdnjAJAS6bogAAYGYvcBBRAx0YYO2Bwdn02-FYshPtRC38g==&ch=ZmFCl_akhXm8xbqOJkQSeDrKbOPqAH6VfWz8PyO4q0FXawlVlK79fw==).

name: Mike Thompson
subject: MLK’s messages still ring true
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress unsubscribe: https://email
body:
# [#]
Peter,

Today marks Martin Luther King, Jr. Day, a solemn holiday for our nation. Today we remember the life and legacy of one of our nation's greatest civil rights leaders. And we redouble our efforts to live our lives guided by his teachings. Our progress forward is not finished, and it's on us to continue that work.

As Dr. King once said “The arc of the moral universe is long, but it bends towards justice.”

Even now, 50 years since his death, we must continue that march toward justice. Particularly now, as we face a year that began with hate and division, we must remember his words and remember that we will rise above when we stand united and demand a better future.

As we take this time to remember the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., we must remember his message of hope in a time of darkness.

Thank you for standing together and continuing our nation's long march toward justice.

Mike Thompson

Friends, The United States represents the world’s best hope for peace and freedom.
| | | | [](https://nicolemalliotakis.lt.acemlnc.com/Prod/link-tracker?redirectUrl=aHR0cHMlM0ElMkYlMkZuaWNvbGVtYWxsaW90YWtpcy5jb20lM0Z1dG1fc291cmNlJTNEZW1haWwlMjZ1dG1fY29udGVudCUzRGVtYWlsJTJCbG9nbyUyNnV0bV9tZWRpdW0lM0RhcHBlYWwlMjZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ24lM0RjaGFsbGVuZ2Vz&a=25864240&account=nicolemalliotakis%2Eactivehosted%2Ecom&email=8RRDsQMegL%2Bk1g7KlTCN33wFoqDlMHNmyq65fGLdufk%3D&s=e671297fc19753f4b1f2ae7718aefd90&i=414A601A1A8221)

| | Friends,

“The ultimate measure of a man is not where he stands in moments of Comfort and Convenience, but where he stands at times of Challenge and Controversy.”

~Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., Strength to Love 1963

These words of Dr. Martin Luther King ring as true today as they did nearly six decades ago.

Dr. King achieved lasting change through his belief in nonviolence and civility. Despite the challenges he faced, Dr. King remained committed to his principles and stood firm through adversity—bringing people together and changing our country forever.

The United States represents the world’s best hope for peace and freedom. While our democracy is being tested by divisions that tear at the fabric our nation, we must remember Dr. King’s lessons, commit ourselves to meeting the challenges of this moment and bring Americans of all walks of life together once again.

On this day of remembrance, let’s focus on restoring civility to the great public debate that is American democracy and embracing Dr. King’s work to change hearts through his loving words. In so doing, we will truly honor Dr. Martin Luther King Jr.’s legacy.

Sincerely,
Nicole Malliotakis
U.S. Representative

Tea Party Patriots Action is working on our plans for the next two and four years.

Please help tell us how Tea Party Patriots Action can better reach our goals, how you would suggest we approach election integrity, how can we better stand against the unconstitutional, un-American legislation that is sure to come out of Congress in the next 100+ days.

We are not giving up, and we are not giving in. We see how the radical left is trying to take advantage of the current unstable environment to press their agenda forward. We oppose their efforts to impeach President Trump. We oppose their efforts to silence and remove Members of Congress who used the Constitution and the law to make known their concerns over election integrity. We oppose Big Tech’s determination to silence conservative voices.

There are still things we can do peacefully within the system. We are working on an action plan for the next two years. These plans will include work to ensure the integrity of future elections, training future leaders so we can be wiser and more effective, and educating Americans on the harms of socialism and the radical left’s socialist agenda. Before finalizing such a plan, we want to hear from you.

name: Greg Stanton
subject: Honoring Dr. King
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stanton for Congress |
body:
Friend,

Dr. King challenged America to fully live up to its values.

He maintained an unshakeable faith in this country and its people: that we could rise above hate to form a "beloved community" rooted in equality and justice for all.

The resilience of Dr. King’s dream inspires me. Throughout his fight, he believed in our country and our ability to come together.

In the midst of so much conflict over the future of America, we must carry Dr. King's legacy forward -- inspired by his words and actions.

We must continue to let his dream be our guide.

In solidarity,

--Greg

name: John Carney
subject: Link for Virtual Inauguration
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends for John Carney
body:
---

Friend,

I wish we could be together for our inauguration, but as you know, we have to celebrate differently this year. I wanted to make sure you had the link to watch our **virtual inauguration ceremony on** **Tuesday, January 19 at 10 a.m.: [de.gov/inauguration](https://act.myngp.com/el/L9XcDt7o1e9mZNE9ghjDFA==/1lqdob0lYvAthjBgL0A5Jw==)**

For the past two decades, Delawareans have trusted me with the responsibility and privilege of representing them as Lieutenant Governor, in the U.S. Congress and now as Governor. Your support has meant so much to me over the years-- and even more so as we faced the challenges of 2020 together.

As we begin a second term, I promise to continue working hard, every single day, on the issues that are important to the people of our state.

Thank you for placing your trust in me to serve as your Governor. I hope you'll join us for our **[virtual inauguration ceremony](https://act.myngp.com/el/L9XcDt7o1e9mZNE9ghjDFA==/1lqdob0lYvAthjBgL0A5Jw==)**.

Sincerely,

John

_Governor John Carney_
_74th Governor, State of Delaware_
